Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following best describes the principle of physical independence of forces?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The physical independence principal states that the effect of a force on a body is not affected by
the presence of the other forces.
So, even if there are a number of forces acting on the same body, each force has its own influence
as other forces was absent.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

A mass of 50 kg is suspended by a rope that is wound 2.5 times around a fixed horizontal rod.
The coefficient of static friction between the rope and the rod is 0.25. What is the minimum force required on the free end of the rope to support the mass in equilibrium? (Take g = 9.81 m/s2)

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A 5 kg block starts from rest on a smooth horizontal floor under the action of a force F = 3t – 2t2, where t is the time in seconds. The maximum speed of the block is _______ m/s. (Correct upto three decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.225
Solution

0.225 (0.220 - 0.230)

 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A block of mass 3 kg falls freely from a height of 2 m on the top of a spring of stiffness 10 N/mm. Spring is compressed by 70 mm. The velocity of the block when the spring is deformed is ____ m/sec. (Correct upto 2 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 4.93
Solution

4.93 (4.80 - 5.20)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A system of six pulleys is used to lift a load of 1038 N. If the efficiency of the pulley system is 90%, the effort P required to lift the load is _______ N. (Correct upto two decimal places)

 
NAT
Correct answer: 192.22
Solution

192.22 (192 - 193)

 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following forces do not perform virtual work during a virtual displacement in a static system?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c, d)
While computing the virtual work, we should remember that there are many forces encountered
in statics which do not work as these forces are applied through fixed points i.e., dr = 0 examples of such forces are:
(i) Reaction at a frictionless pin; body rotating about the pin.
(ii) Reaction at a frictionless contacting surface when two bodies are in relative motion.
(iii) Reaction at a roller moving along its track.
(iv) Weight of a body when its CG moves horizontally.
(v) Friction force acting on a wheel rolling without slipping as the point of contact is instantaneously it rest.

A structure with pin joined members is shown in the figure. The value of load W is 50 kN and θ is 25°. The value of force P required to keep the system in equilibrium is ______ kN. [Neglect friction at the joints and weight of the members] [Round off up to three decimal places]

 
NAT
Correct answer: 53.613
Solution

53.613 (53 - 54)

 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

The length of a 4 kg bar is 6 meters. The floor and wall are smooth. The spring is unstretched when the angle α = 0°. If the bar is in equilibrium at α = 30°, then the spring constant is_____N/m.
[Correct upto 2 decimal points]

 
NAT
Correct answer: 24.88
Solution

24.88 (23.00 - 26.00)

NAT
Correct answer: 73.33
Solution
